name: Josephine Cochrane's Dishwasher
short_description: The first patented mechanical dishwashing machine, using water-jet pressure and wire compartments to automate the washing of dishes — the origin of purpose-built domestic cleaning appliances.
long_description: Patented by Josephine Cochrane in 1886 (US Patent 355,139) and demonstrated to wide acclaim at the 1893 World's Columbian Exposition in Chicago, this machine automated a household chore that had always been done by hand. Wire compartments sized for plates, cups, and saucers were mounted in a flat wheel inside a copper boiler; a motor rotated the wheel while hot soapy water was pumped up from the bottom and rained down on the dishes — the first design to clean by water pressure rather than mechanical scrubbers. Commercial establishments (hotels, restaurants) adopted it first because early models required hot-water infrastructure and cost too much for most households. Cochrane founded the Garis-Cochrane Manufacturing Company to produce it; the company was eventually acquired by Hobart Manufacturing and the product line later became part of KitchenAid. As the origin point on the domestic-labor spectrum, it shows how a fixed, single-task machine can replace a repetitive physical chore entirely — the design logic that still governs purpose-built home appliances today.
ai_note: Purely mechanical: a motor-driven rotating rack inside a copper boiler, with a pump that jets hot water. No computation, sensing, or machine learning of any kind.

images.caption: Patent drawing for Josephine Cochrane's dishwashing machine (US Patent 1,223,380, 1917), showing the rotating wire-rack wheel, copper boiler, and water-jet mechanism that descends from her original 1886 design.
images.alt_text: A black-and-white technical patent drawing of an early mechanical dishwashing machine, showing a cylindrical boiler with a rotating rack wheel inside and water-pump plumbing labeled with figure numbers.

sources.title: The Woman Who Invented the Dishwasher
sources.access_notes: Overview of Cochrane's invention, 1886 patent, World's Columbian Exposition win, company founding, and use of water pressure over scrubbers.

sources.title: Josephine Cochrane
sources.access_notes: Patent No. 355,139 issued December 28, 1886; mechanical details (wire compartments, copper boiler, rotating wheel, water-jet pump); company history; acquisition by Hobart and eventual KitchenAid lineage.

sources.title: Dishwasher — History
sources.access_notes: Contextualizes Cochrane's 1886 machine as the first to use water pressure rather than scrubbers; notes George Butters as co-builder; 1893 Columbian Exposition debut.
